response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills across 15+ years, there are multiple critical structural mismatches with the role. Most significantly, native Japanese language proficiency is explicitly required but completely absent from the resume. The candidate's C programming experience ended in 2014-2015 (6 months at National Instruments) and is insufficient for modern embedded firmware development. Recent career trajectory (2+ years of TypeScript/Node.js web development) has moved away from systems-level work. The role requires deep embedded firmware expertise, Linux kernel knowledge, and networking/security specialization—areas where this full-stack web engineer shows no demonstrated experience.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software engineering experience with proven ability to master multiple languages and domains
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and demonstrated mentoring/teaching ability across multiple organizations
Some exposure to hardware and embedded systems through robotics work at Altom and hardware control projects, showing adaptability
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency despite this being an explicit core requirement for the role
C programming expertise is severely outdated (last significant C work ended 2014-2015); recent 7+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web development, not embedded systems
No demonstrated Linux kernel expertise, embedded firmware development experience, or security/networking background—the core technical domain of this role

key_factors.factor: Modern C and embedded firmware expertise
Linux kernel and security/networking domain knowledge
